info.name: College Management System
info.description: Complete API collection for the College Management System microservices. All requests go through the API Gateway at http://localhost:8080. Protected endpoints require a Bearer token in the Authorization header.

request.description:
This API is used to authenticate a user using email and password.

On successful authentication, it returns a JWT token which is required to access all protected APIs in the system.

The token is automatically stored in the Postman collection variable `{{token}}` for reuse in subsequent requests.

**What it does:**

- Validates user credentials
    
- Generates JWT token
    
- Returns user identity details (username, universityId, role context)
    

**Success (200):**

- Returns JWT token
    
- Token is used for authorization in all secured APIs
    

**Failure Cases:**

- 401 → Invalid email or password
    
- 400 → Missing required fields (email/password)
    

StartFragment

**Login**

**POST** /api/auth/login

Authenticates user and returns JWT token.

**Request**

{

"email": "[admin@college.edu](https://mailto:admin@college.edu)",

"password": "Admin@1234"

}

**Response:-**

**Success:**

{  
"token": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9...",  
"username": "admin",  
"id": 1,  
"universityId": "ADM-2024-001",  
"email": "[admin@college.edu](https://mailto:admin@college.edu)",  
"type": "Bearer",  
"expiresIn": 86400000,  
"issuedAt": "2024-01-15T10:00:00",  
"expiresAt": "2024-01-16T10:00:00",  
"timeStamp": "2024-01-15T10:00:00"  
}

**Errors:**

- {  
    "status": 401,  
    "error": "Unauthorized",  
    "message": "Invalid email or password",  
    "timestamp": "2024-01-15T10:00:00"  
    }
    
- {  
    "status": 400,  
    "error": "Bad Request",  
    "message": "Email and password are required",  
    "timestamp": "2024-01-15T10:00:00"  
    }

request.description:
**Description:**

This API is used to logout a user by invalidating (blacklisting) the current JWT token.

After logout, the same token cannot be used again for accessing protected APIs.

**What it does:**

- Marks the token as invalid in the system
    
- Ensures secure session termination
    

**Headers Required:**

- Authorization: Bearer token
    
- X-User-Name: username
    

**Success (200):**

- Token is successfully invalidated
    
- User session is terminated
    

**Failure Cases:**

- 401 → Missing or invalid token
    

StartFragment

**Logout**

**POST** /api/auth/logout  
Invalidates the current token.

**Headers**

Authorization: Bearer {{token}}

X-User-Name: username

EndFragment

**Success :**

{  
"status": 200,  
"message": "Logged out successfully",  
"timestamp": "2024-01-15T10:00:00"  
}

**Error:**

{  
"status": 401,  
"message": "Unauthorized – missing or invalid token"  
}

request.description:
StartFragment

**Description:**

This API checks whether a given JWT token is still valid or has been expired/blacklisted.

Used internally by API Gateway and services to verify authentication.

**What it does:**

-   
    Validates token authenticity  
    
-   
    Checks expiration and blacklist status  
    

**Success (200):**

- `true` → Token is valid  
    
- `false` → Token is invalid or expired  
    

**Failure Cases:**

-   
    Invalid token format may return false  
    

---

EndFragment

request.description:
**Description:**

This API initiates the password reset process.

It sends a reset token or instructions (OTP/email link) to the user’s registered email.

**What it does:**

- Verifies if email exists
    
- Generates reset token
    
- Sends reset instructions to email
    

**Success (200):**

- Reset instructions sent successfully
    

**Failure Cases:**

- 404 → Email not registered in system
    

Forgot Password:-

StartFragment

**POST** /api/auth/forgot-password

Sends reset instructions to email.

EndFragment

Success :  
{  
"status": 200,  
"message": "Password reset instructions sent to email"  
}  
Error:

{  
"status": 404,  
"message": "No user found with this email"  
}

request.description:
**Description:**

This API generates and sends an OTP to the user as part of the password reset flow.

It requires a valid reset token obtained from the forgot-password step.

**What it does:**

- Validates reset token
    
- Generates OTP
    
- Sends OTP to user
    

**Success (200):**

- OTP sent successfully
    

**Failure Cases:**

- 400 → Invalid or expired reset token
    

StartFragment

**POST** /api/auth/generate-otp?token=RESET_TOKEN

EndFragment

Success:

{  
"status": 200,  
"message": "OTP sent successfully"  
}  
Error:

{  
"status": 400,  
"message": "Invalid or expired reset token"  
}

request.description:
**Description:**

This API verifies the OTP entered by the user during password reset flow.

**What it does:**

-   
    Validates OTP against token  
    
-   
    Confirms user identity before password reset  
    

**Success (200):**

-   
    OTP verified successfully  
    

**Failure Cases:**

-   
    400 → Invalid OTP  
    
-   
    410 → OTP expired
    

  
  

StartFragment

**POST** /api/auth/verify-otp

**Request**

{

"token": "RESET_TOKEN",

"otp": 123456

}

EndFragment

Success:

{  
"status": 200,  
"message": "OTP verified successfully"  
}  
Error:

{  
"status": 400,  
"message": "Invalid OTP"  
}

{  
"status": 410,  
"message": "OTP has expired. Please request a new one."  
}

request.description:
**Description:**

This API allows the user to reset their password after successful OTP verification.

**What it does:**

-   
    Validates token + OTP flow completion  
    
-   
    Updates user password securely  
    

**Success (200):**

-   
    Password reset successfully  
    

**Failure Cases:**

-   
    400 → Passwords do not match  
    
-   
    400 → New password same as old password
    

  
  

Reset Password :-

StartFragment

**POST** /api/auth/reset-password

EndFragment  
Success:

{  
"status": 200,  
"message": "Password reset successfully"  
}

Error:

{  
"status": 400,  
"message": "Passwords do not match"  
}

{  
"status": 400,  
"message": "New password cannot be same as old password"  
}

request.description:
StartFragment

**Description:**

This API is used to create a new user in the system.

It supports multiple roles such as ADMIN, FACULTY, and LIBRARIAN.

Only ADMIN users are allowed to create new users.

**What it does:**

- Validates input data
    
- Creates user with role-based fields
    
- Triggers cascade creation in respective services (Student/Faculty/Library)
    
- Stores user with unique universityId
    

**Headers Required:**

- Authorization: Bearer token
    
- X-User-Role: ADMIN
    
- X-User-Name: username
    

**Success (201):**

- User created successfully
    
- Returns user details including universityId
    

**Failure Cases:**

- 400 → Validation errors (invalid email, weak password, missing fields)
    
- 409 → User already exists (email or universityId duplicate)
    
- 403 → Only ADMIN can create users
    

EndFragment

StartFragment

**POST** /api/users

Creates a new user (ADMIN only)

**Headers**

Authorization: Bearer token

X-User-Role: ADMIN

**Request**

{

"email": "[john.doe@college.edu](https://mailto:john.doe@college.edu)",  
"username": "johndoe",  
"name": "John Doe",  
"role": "FACULTY",  
"department": "Computer Science",  
"phoneNumber": "9876543210",  
"universityId": "FAC-000001"

}

EndFragment

Success:  
{  
"status": 201,  
"message": "User created successfully",  
"username": "[john.doe@college.edu](https://mailto:john.doe@college.edu)",  
"data": {  
"id": 5,  
"email": "[john.doe@college.edu](https://mailto:john.doe@college.edu)",  
"username": "johndoe",  
"name": "John Doe",  
"role": "FACULTY",  
"department": "Computer Science",  
"phoneNumber": "9876543210",  
"universityId": "FAC-000001",  
"active": true,  
"createdAt": "2024-01-15T10:00:00"  
},  
"timestamp": "2024-01-15T10:00:00"  
}

Error:

{  
"status": 400,  
"message": "Validation failed",  
"errors": {  
"email": "Email should be valid",  
"password": "Password must be at least 8 characters"  
}  
}

{  
"status": 409,  
"message": "User with this email or universityId already exists"  
}

{  
"status": 403,  
"message": "Only ADMIN can create users"  
}

request.description:
**Description:**

This API fetches all users in the system.

You can optionally filter users by role.

**What it does:**

- Retrieves list of users
    
- Supports filtering using query parameter `role`
    

**Query Param (Optional):**

- role = ADMIN / STUDENT / FACULTY / LIBRARIAN
    

**Headers Required:**

- Authorization: Bearer token
    
- X-User-Role: ADMIN
    

**Success (200):**

- Returns list of users
    

**Failure Cases:**

- 403 → Only ADMIN can access all users
    

---

StartFragment

**GET** /api/users?role=STUDENT

Returns users (ADMIN only)

EndFragment

Success:

{  
"status": 200,  
"message": "Users fetched successfully",  
"data": \[  
{ "id": 1,

"email": "...",

"name": "...",

"role": "STUDENT",

"universityId": "STU-2024-001",

"active": true

}  
\]  
}

Error:

{  
"status": 403,  
"message": "Only ADMIN can view all users"  
}

This API retrieves a specific user using their universityId.  
  
This is preferred over database ID because universityId is used across all microservices.

**What it does:**

-   
    Fetches user details by business identifier (universityId)  
    

**Headers Required:**

-   
    Authorization: Bearer token  
    

**Success (200):**

-   
    Returns user details  
    

**Failure Cases:**

-   
    404 → User not found with given universityId

{  
"status": 200,  
"message": "User fetched successfully",  
"data": { "id": 5, "universityId": "STU-2024-001", "email": "[john@college.edu](https://mailto:john@college.edu)" }  
}

Error:

{  
"status": 404,  
"message": "User not found with universityId: STU-2024-001"  
}

request.description:
StartFragment

**Description:**

This API performs a soft delete of a user.  
  
Instead of removing the user permanently, it marks the user as inactive.

**What it does:**

-   
    Sets user status to inactive  
    
-   
    Keeps data for audit and recovery purposes  
    

**Headers Required:**

-   
    Authorization: Bearer token  
    
-   
    X-User-Role: ADMIN  
    

**Success (200):**

-   
    User deleted (soft delete)  
    

**Failure Cases:**

-   
    404 → User not found  
    
-   
    403 → Only ADMIN can delete users
    

EndFragment  
  

success:

{  
"status": 200,  
"message": "User deleted successfully",  
"reason": "Soft delete applied"  
}

Error:

{  
"status": 404,  
"message": "User not found"  
}

**Description:**

This API is used to create a new department in the system (e.g., Computer Science, Mechanical, etc.).

Each department must have a unique department code.

Only ADMIN users are allowed to create departments.

**What it does:**

- Validates department name and code
    
- Creates a new department record
    
- Ensures uniqueness of departmentCode
    

**Headers Required:**

- Authorization: Bearer token
    
- X-User-Role: ADMIN
    

**Success (201):**

- Department created successfully
    

**Failure Cases:**

- 400 → Invalid input data
    
- 409 → Department code already exists
    
- 403 → Only ADMIN can create departments
    

EndFragment

Returns system-wide stats: total students, faculty, departments, courses, library stats.  
  

## Admin Dashboard

**Endpoint:** `GET /api/dashboard/admin`

**Description:**

This API provides overall system analytics for admin users.

**What it does:**

-   
    Returns system-wide statistics:  
    
    -   
        Total users  
        
    -   
        Students count  
        
    -   
        Faculty count  
        
    -   
        Courses  
        
    -   
        Departments  
        
    -   
        Library stats  
        

**Headers Required:**

-   
    Authorization: Bearer token  
    
-   
    X-User-Role: ADMIN  
    

**Success (200):**

-   
    Admin dashboard data returned  
    

**Failure Cases:**

-   
    403 → Only ADMIN allowed  
    
-   
    401 → Unauthorized

## Student Dashboard

**Endpoint:** `GET /api/dashboard/student/{universityId}`

**Description:**

This API provides dashboard data for a student.

**What it does:**

-   
    Fetches student-related data:  
    
    -   
        Subjects  
        
    -   
        Attendance summary  
        
    -   
        Academic details  
        
    -   
        Notifications (if any)  
        

**Path Param:**

-   
    universityId → unique student identifier  
    

**Headers Required:**

-   
    Authorization: Bearer token  
    

**Success (200):**

-   
    Returns complete student dashboard  
    

**Failure Cases:**

-   
    404 → Student not found  
    
-   
    401 → Unauthorized

Returns the profile of the currently logged-in user based on X-User-Name, X-User-Role, and universityId headers.

### Get My Profile

**Endpoint:** `GET /api/profile/me`

**Description:**

This API retrieves the logged-in user’s profile details using headers passed from the API Gateway.

This endpoint does not require a request body because user identity is extracted from headers populated via JWT.

**What it does:**

- Reads user identity from headers
    
- Fetches profile data based on:
    
    - username
        
    - role
        
    - universityId
        
- Returns user-specific profile details
    

**Headers Required:**

- X-User-Name
    
- X-User-Role
    
- X-University-Id
    
- Authorization: Bearer token
    

**Success (200):**

- Returns user profile data
    

**Failure Cases:**

- 401 → Missing or invalid token
    
- 400 → Required headers missing

## Generic Role-Based Dashboard

**Endpoint:** `GET /api/dashboard/role/{role}/{userId}`

**Description:**

This API provides a generic dashboard based on role.

However, it is recommended to use specific endpoints (student, faculty, etc.) instead of this one.

**What it does:**

- Dynamically fetches dashboard data based on role
    
- Acts as a fallback/general-purpose endpoint
    

**Path Params:**

- role → STUDENT / FACULTY / LIBRARIAN / ADMIN
    
- userId → universityId
    

**Headers Required:**

- Authorization: Bearer token
    

**Success (200):**

- Returns role-based dashboard data
    

**Failure Cases:**

- 400 → Invalid role
    
- 404 → User not found
